title: The Positron cannon in Episode 22
wikitext:
[]: The Positron cannon in Episode 22             Quote Post by Replicator » Tue Jan 15, 2013 9:39 am What was powering the Positron Cannon used against Arael in Episode 22? The last time it was put into action it needed all of the electricity in Japan, so how did they even manage to power up without busting everyone's lights? Or was it only in 'low power mode', which might explain why it couldn't be effective?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jan 15, 2013 9:50 am I don't think it was so much that it needed all that power in order to operate , more that it needed all that power to get through Ramiel's ATF.

[]:             Quote Post by Lance of LoL » Tue Jan 15, 2013 10:20 am Ornette wrote: I don't think it was so much that it needed all that power in order to operate , more that it needed all that power to get through Ramiel's ATF.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jan 15, 2013 2:15 pm Lance of LoL wrote: That's exactly it, they needed a certain level of charge in order to pierce Ramiel's defence. Why they thought it would work at such a distance against Arael with less power before they knew what its AT Field actually did is beyond me.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jan 16, 2013 4:46 pm Nerv didn't assume that they would need more power than what they had used to break through the A.T. Field.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Fri Jan 25, 2013 9:56 pm I would say in the spirit of Eva the angel would be created with a weaker AT field since it is so far away. Or the gun was the best they could do given the time constraints of first seeing the angel

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Feb 03, 2013 3:01 am They had a fair amount of time to set everything up to power the positron cannon against Ramiel. They didn't know the full strength of Arael's ATF and didn't have enough time to set up Operation Yashima again (since Asuka was under attack) so I suppose they were hoping the Angel would have a weaker AT Field. Or possibly Gendo just didn't have them use a fully-powered positron cannon so that they would be forced to use the Spear of Longinus, stalling Seele's original scenario.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Feb 03, 2013 3:53 pm The obvious solution is that the cannon used in Operation Yashima is a prototype, and the one used by Asuka is the more energy-efficient final version.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Mon Feb 04, 2013 6:44 pm Of course the electric balls shot by the newer positron rifle weren't as powerful as those charged by the entire nation in Ramiel's battle: remember Asuka firing at random during the mind rape scene, those shots caused only moderate explosions; the one single shot to Ramiel, which curved and fell to the ground, brought on a maelstrom of energy.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 04, 2013 7:26 pm xdiesp wrote: Of course the electric balls shot by the newer positron rifle weren't as powerful as those charged by the entire nation in Ramiel's battle: remember Asuka firing at random during the mind rape scene, those shots caused only moderate explosions; the one single shot to Ramiel, which curved and fell to the ground, brought on a maelstrom of energy.

[]:             Quote Post by caragnafog dog » Wed Feb 13, 2013 11:55 am Always thought it was strange that there were angel's weak enough and man-made weapons strong enough to kill angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 13, 2013 2:53 pm The Evas' knives and guns are man-made. Are you referring to those?

[]:             Quote Post by caragnafog dog » Wed Feb 13, 2013 8:56 pm nah, as talking about the positron cannon. it would be more accurate if I said that what exactly could or couldn't kill angels always confused me.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Thu Feb 14, 2013 2:44 am Speaking of the guns, I'm kind of surprised they kept on even giving the EVAs those rifles after a while. They were probably the least effective weapons they had in the series. The Progressive Knife seems to have been a top tier weapon though, lol.

[]: Not that useless. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Feb 14, 2013 4:37 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Speaking of the guns, I'm kind of surprised they kept on even giving the EVAs those rifles after a while.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Thu Feb 14, 2013 4:45 am Agh, forgot about Matariel.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Thu Feb 14, 2013 10:22 am Did that even count as a battle? Classic "crap we have 2 minutes to kill this thing...uhhh...shoot it? yeah lets shoot it!"

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu Feb 14, 2013 11:05 am sum_exsanguis wrote: Did that even count as a battle? Classic "crap we have 2 minutes to kill this thing...uhhh...shoot it? yeah lets shoot it!"

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Feb 14, 2013 5:33 pm Well, those rifles were built with those monsters in mind, not surprising that it worked out in a few cases at least. And positrons are technically antimatter, and Angels, as powerful as they may be, are living flesh and blood things. The former punching a hole in the latter seems convincing enough to me. There's nothing that can't be solved with sufficient qualities of brute force. If you can't solve something with brute force, you simply don't have enough brute force at your disposal. But the kind of brute force that would destroy the likes of say, Zeruel, simply wasn't invented yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Feb 14, 2013 5:59 pm Kendrix wrote: And positrons are technically antimatter, and Angels, as powerful as they may be, are living flesh and blood things. The former punching a hole in the latter seems convincing enough to me.

The last time it was put into action it needed all of the electricity in Japan, so how did they even manage to power up without busting everyone's lights? Or was it only in 'low power mode', which might explain why it couldn't be effective?
